mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This is an always-on signal reflecting underlying economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth leading up to 2007-11-15
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates significantly, it erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to investor uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns as the Federal Reserve is pressured to tighten monetary policy.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL value of 276.724 suggests ongoing inflation
failure_condition: If, during periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining housing starts and extremely low federal funds rates indicates a severe housing market downturn prompting aggressive monetary easing. This environment suggests significant economic distress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns despite low rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08 is exceptionally low, suggesting distress
regime_hint: housing market contraction with aggressive monetary easing
failure_condition: If, when HOUST declines by more than 10% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a rising unemployment rate signals weakening consumer demand and corporate profitability, leading to sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when the economy is already in a downturn.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6 is currently low, but a sharp rise would be concerning
failure_condition: If, during an identified economic contraction, a 0.5pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth rate exceeds 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS value of 625416.0 suggests healthy consumption
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month average growth exceeds 0.5% MoM, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall supply-side capacity expansion, which underpins corporate profitability and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value of 100.0178 suggests stable production
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive growth but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant reduction in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, which aims to stimulate economic activity and lower the cost of capital, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 50 basis points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08 is already very low, implying further cuts are limited but significant if they occur
failure_condition: If, following a FEDFUNDS drop of more than 50bps in a month, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating GDP growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates a significant economic slowdown or recession, which severely impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1.0% AND UNRATE rises by 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19465.195 suggests growth, but deceleration would be a concern
failure_condition: If, when GDPC1 annualized growth is below 1.0% and UNRATE rises by 0.3pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.
